979	\subsubsection{Module-structure transport helpers (reindex)}
980	\label{sec:gf_reindex_transport_helpers}
981	
982	The localisation-transport step of \cref{lem:gf_torsion_reindex} moves a module structure
983	and its finiteness across (i) an additive equivalence of the underlying groups, (ii) a ring
984	isomorphism of the acting ring, and (iii) a change of localising monoid along a scalar
985	tower. The following helper lemmas package those three transport principles. They are general
986	module-theory facts; no external source is cited.
987	
988	\begin{lemma}\leanok
989	[Module transport along an additive equivalence]
990	  \label{lem:gf_pullback_module_transport}
991	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.pullbackModuleAddEquiv}
992	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv}
993	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.pullback_isScalarTower}
994	  Let \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} N\) be an additive equivalence and let \(M\) carry an
995	  \(R\)-module structure. Pulling the action back along \(e\) by
996	  \(r \cdot y := e\big(r \cdot e^{-1}(y)\big)\) makes \(N\) an \(R\)-module for which \(e\)
997	  is \(R\)-linear. Under this transported structure: (a) if \(M\) is module-finite over
998	  \(R\) then so is \(N\); and (b) if \(M\) additionally carries a compatible action of a
999	  base ring \(A_g\) with scalar tower \(A_g \to R \to M\), then the pulled-back actions on
1000	  \(N\) again form a scalar tower \(A_g \to R \to N\).
1001	\end{lemma}
1002	\begin{proof}
1003	  The module axioms for \(y \mapsto e(r \cdot e^{-1}(y))\) follow termwise from those on
1004	  \(M\) together with the fact that \(e\) and \(e^{-1}\) are additive and mutually inverse;
1005	  by construction \(e\) is then an \(R\)-linear isomorphism. Statement (a) follows by
1006	  transporting module-finiteness across the \(R\)-linear isomorphism \(e\).
1007	  Statement (b) checks the scalar-tower identity
1008	  \((a\,r)\cdot y = a\cdot(r\cdot y)\) on \(N\) by transporting it across \(e\) and applying
1009	  associativity of the scalar action on \(M\).
1010	\end{proof}
1011	
1012	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1013	[Finite transport along a ring isomorphism of the acting ring]
1014	  \label{lem:gf_finite_of_quotient_ringequiv}
1015	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.finite_of_quotientRingEquiv}
1016	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring and let \(\psi : B_1 \xrightarrow{\sim} B_2\) be a ring
1017	  isomorphism between two \(R\)-algebras that is compatible with the structure maps, i.e.\
1018	  \(\psi \circ (\text{algebra map } R \to B_1) = (\text{algebra map } R \to B_2)\). If
1019	  \(B_2\) is module-finite over \(R\) and \(M\) is module-finite over \(B_1\) (with the
1020	  scalar tower \(R \to B_1 \to M\)), then \(M\) is module-finite over \(R\).
1021	\end{lemma}
1022	\begin{proof}
1023	  Compatibility of \(\psi^{-1}\) with the structure maps follows from that of \(\psi\), so
1024	  \(\psi^{-1}\) is an \(R\)-algebra isomorphism; transporting \(R\)-finiteness of \(B_2\)
1025	  across it makes \(B_1\) module-finite over \(R\). Transitivity of module-finiteness through
1026	  the scalar tower \(R \to B_1 \to M\) then gives \(M\) module-finite over \(R\).
1027	\end{proof}
1028	
1029	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1030	[Descent of a localised-module structure along a scalar tower]
1031	  \label{lem:gf_islocalizedmodule_restrictscalars}
1032	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars}
1033	  Let \(A_g \to R\) be a ring map with compatible \(A_g\)- and \(R\)-module structures on
1034	  \(M\) and \(M'\) (scalar towers \(A_g \to R \to M\) and \(A_g \to R \to M'\)), and let
1035	  \(S \subseteq A_g\) be a multiplicative subset. If an \(R\)-linear map
1036	  \(f : M \to M'\) realises \(M'\) as the localisation of \(M\) at the image submonoid
1037	  \(S \cdot 1 \subseteq R\) over \(R\), then its restriction of scalars to \(A_g\) realises
1038	  \(M'\) as the localisation of \(M\) at \(S\) over \(A_g\).
1039	\end{lemma}
1040	\begin{proof}
1041	  One verifies the universal property of a localised module for the restricted map. Each
1042	  \(s \in S\) acts invertibly on \(M'\): its action coincides, via the scalar tower, with
1043	  that of its image in \(R\), which is a unit because \(f\) is a localisation at the image
1044	  submonoid. Surjectivity onto \(M'\) by fractions \(x/s\) with \(s \in S\) and the
1045	  equality criterion likewise descend from the corresponding image-submonoid statements,
1046	  rewriting each \(R\)-scalar by an \(A_g\)-scalar through the scalar tower. Assembling
1047	  these conditions gives the claim.
1048	\end{proof}

1050	\begin{lemma}[Localisation identification across an associated denominator]
1051	  \label{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated}
1052	  \lean{IsLocalization.Away.mul_of_associated}
1053	  \mathlibok
1054	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1055	  Let \(R \to S \to T\) be a tower of commutative rings with compatible structure maps, let
1056	  \(x, z \in R\) and \(y \in S\), and suppose \(S\) is the localisation of \(R\) away from
1057	  \(x\) and \(T\) is the localisation of \(S\) away from \(y\). If the image of \(z\) in \(S\)
1058	  is associated to \(y\) (the two differ by a unit), then \(T\) is the localisation of \(R\)
1059	  away from the single product \(x z\); that is, \(T \cong R[(x z)^{-1}]\).
1060	\end{lemma}

1062	\begin{lemma}[Freeness transports across a semilinear ring isomorphism]
1063	  \label{lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1064	  \lean{Module.Free.of_ringEquiv}
1065	  \mathlibok
1066	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1067	  Let \(R, R'\) be rings, \(M\) an \(R\)-module and \(M'\) an \(R'\)-module, and let
1068	  \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} M'\) be an additive isomorphism that is semilinear over a ring
1069	  isomorphism \(\sigma : R \xrightarrow{\sim} R'\), i.e.\ \(e(r \cdot m) = \sigma(r) \cdot e(m)\).
1070	  If \(M\) is free over \(R\), then \(M'\) is free over \(R'\).
1071	\end{lemma}

1073	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1074	[L5 step-4 helper — descent of freeness across a tower of \(\mathrm{Away}\) localisations]
1075	  \label{lem:gf_away_tower_descent}
1076	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.free_localizationAway_of_away_tower}
1077	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated, lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1078	  Let \(A\) be a domain and \(T\) an \(A\)-module. Let \(g \in A\), \(g \ne 0\), and let
1079	  \(h \in A_g := \mathrm{Away}(g)\) be non-zero. Write
1080	  \((T_g)_h\) for the iterated localisation of \(T\) — first away from \(g\), then away from
1081	  \(h\) — and \((A_g)_h := \mathrm{Away}(h)\) for the corresponding iterated localisation of the
1082	  base ring. If \((T_g)_h\) is free over \((A_g)_h\), then there exists \(f \in A\),
1083	  \(f \ne 0\), such that the single localisation \(T_f\) is free over \(A_f := \mathrm{Away}(f)\).
1084	  Concretely the witness is the single product \(f := g\,a\) for an element \(a \in A\) whose
1085	  image in \(A_g\) is associated to \(h\).
1086	\end{lemma}
1087	
1088	\begin{proof}
1089	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated, lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}
1090	  \emph{Clear the denominator of \(h\).} As \(h\) lies in \(A_g = \mathrm{Away}(g)\),
1091	  surjectivity of the localisation map \(A \to A_g\) onto fractions yields \(a \in A\) and a
1092	  power \(s\) of \(g\) with \(h \cdot \bar s = \bar a\) in \(A_g\), where \(\bar{\,\cdot\,}\)
1093	  denotes the image in \(A_g\). The image \(\bar s\) is a unit (a power of \(g\) is invertible
1094	  in \(A_g\)), so \(\bar a = h\,\bar s\) is associated to \(h\); in particular \(\bar a \ne 0\)
1095	  (because \(h \ne 0\)), hence \(a \ne 0\). Since \(A\) is a domain and \(g, a \ne 0\), the
1096	  product \(f := g\,a\) is non-zero.
1097	
1098	  \emph{Identify the base rings.} The associatedness of \(\bar a\) and \(h\), together with the
1099	  tower \(A \to A_g \to A_h\), identifies the iterated localisation
1100	  \((A_g)_h = \mathrm{Away}(h)\) with the single localisation of \(A\) away from the product
1101	  \(g\,a\): by \cref{lem:isLocalization_away_mul_of_associated} (with \(x = g\), \(z = a\),
1102	  \(y = h\)) the ring \(A_h\) is a localisation of \(A\) away from \(g\,a\), and uniqueness of
1103	  localisation furnishes a ring isomorphism \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a) \xrightarrow{\sim} A_h\).
1104	
1105	  \emph{Identify the modules.} The composite localisation map
1106	  \[
1107	    \psi : T \longrightarrow (T_g)_h, \qquad
1108	    \psi := \mathrm{mk}_{\langle h\rangle} \circ \mathrm{mk}_{\langle g\rangle},
1109	  \]
1110	  first into \(T_g\) and then into \((T_g)_h\), realises \((T_g)_h\) as the localisation of
1111	  \(T\) at the powers of \(g\,a\): every \(g^n a^m\) acts invertibly on \((T_g)_h\), \(\psi\) is
1112	  surjective up to such denominators, and its kernel is the expected one. This
1113	  localisation-of-localisation identity is verified directly from the defining properties of a
1114	  localised module; no packaged lemma supplies it.
1115	
1116	  \emph{Transport freeness.} By hypothesis \((T_g)_h\) is free over \(A_h\). The ring
1117	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a) \cong A_h\) and the induced semilinear module equivalence
1118	  \(T_{g\,a} \simeq (T_g)_h\) — assembled from the localised-module identification of the
1119	  previous step and promoted to an \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a)\)-linear equivalence — carry this
1120	  freeness back by \cref{lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv}: \(T_{g\,a}\) is free over
1121	  \(\mathrm{Away}(g\,a)\). Taking \(f := g\,a\) gives \(T_f\) free over \(A_f\), as required.
1122	\end{proof}
1123	
1124	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1125	[L5 — polynomial-ring core of generic freeness]
1126	  \label{lem:gf_polynomial_core}
1127	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial}
1128	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module, lem:gf_splice_shortExact, lem:gf_torsion_base,
1129	        lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport, lem:gf_generic_rank_ses,
1130	        lem:gf_torsion_reindex, lem:gf_away_tower_descent}
1131	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step
1132	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1760-L1772).
1133	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1134	  % "Let $m$ be the generic rank of the finite module $B_g$ over the domain
1135	  %  $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Then we have a short exact sequence of
1136	  %  $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$-modules of the form
1137	  %  $0\to A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0$ where $T$ is a finite
1138	  %  torsion module over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Therefore, the dimension of the
1139	  %  support of $K\otimes_{A_g}T$ as a $K\otimes_{A_g}(B_g)$-module is strictly less
1140	  %  than $n$. Hence by induction on $n$ (applied to the data $A_g$, $B_g$, $T$),
1141	  %  there exists some $h\ne 0$ in $A$ with $T_h$ free over $A_{gh}$. Taking $f=gh$,
1142	  %  the lemma follows from the above short exact sequence."
1143	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (polynomial-ring
1144	  core of the induction).}
1145	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain and \(d \ge 0\). Let \(N\) be a finite module over
1146	  the polynomial ring \(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\), regarded as an \(A\)-module. Then there
1147	  exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
1148	
1149	\end{lemma}
1150	
1151	\begin{proof}
1152	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module, lem:gf_splice_shortExact, lem:gf_torsion_base,
1153	        lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport, lem:gf_generic_rank_ses,
1154	        lem:gf_torsion_reindex, lem:gf_away_tower_descent}
1155	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1760-L1772).
1156	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (polynomial-ring core).}
1157	  We argue by strong induction on \(d\), \emph{generalising the base domain \(A\)} (along
1158	  with the module \(N\)): the inductive hypothesis is available for every \(d' < d\),
1159	  every noetherian domain in place of \(A\), and every finite module over the
1160	  corresponding polynomial ring.
1161	
1162	  \emph{Base case \(d = 0\).} The ring \(P_0 = A[X_1, \dots, X_0]\) is \(A\) itself, so
1163	  \(N\) is a finite \(A\)-module and the claim is exactly the finite-module leaf
1164	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_module}.
1165	
1166	  \emph{Inductive step \(d \ge 1\).} If \(N\) is already a torsion \(P_d\)-module over
1167	  \(A\) — equivalently \(N_K = 0\) for \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) — then the torsion base
1168	  case \cref{lem:gf_torsion_base} produces \(f \ne 0\) with \(N_f = 0\), which is free
1169	  over \(A_f\). Otherwise we proceed in five steps.
1170	  \begin{enumerate}
1171	    \item \textbf{Generic-rank SES.} Apply \cref{lem:gf_generic_rank_ses} to \(N\) over
1172	      \(P_d = A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\): there are \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) and an injective
1173	      \(P_d\)-linear map \(\varphi : P_d^{\oplus m} \to N\) with torsion cokernel
1174	      \(T := N / \operatorname{im}\varphi\), i.e.\ a short exact sequence of
1175	      \(P_d\)-modules
1176	      \[
1177	        0 \to P_d^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\ \varphi\ } N \to T \to 0,
1178	        \qquad T \text{ torsion}.
1179	      \]
1180	      No element of \(A\) is inverted at this step.
1181	    \item \textbf{Reindex the torsion cokernel.} Apply \cref{lem:gf_torsion_reindex} to the
1182	      finite torsion module \(T\): there are \(g \ne 0\) in \(A\) and \(m' < d\) such that
1183	      the localisation \(T_g\) is module-finite over the polynomial ring
1184	      \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\) in fewer variables over the base ring \(A_g = A[g^{-1}]\).
1185	    \item \textbf{Inductive hypothesis at the new base \(A_g\).} Since \(A_g\) is again a
1186	      noetherian domain and \(m' < d\), the inductive hypothesis applies \emph{at base
1187	      \(A_g\)} to the finite \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_{m'}]\)-module \(T_g\): there is
1188	      \(h \ne 0\) in \(A_g\) with \((T_g)_h\) free over \((A_g)_h\). This is exactly the
1189	      point at which the base ring of the induction must be allowed to change from \(A\)
1190	      to \(A_g\).
1191	    \item \textbf{Descend the witness from \(A_g\) to \(A\).} Clearing the denominator of
1192	      \(h\) over \(A_g\) produces a numerator \(a \in A\) whose image in \(A_g\) is associated
1193	      to \(h\); then the iterated localisation \((A_g)_h\) is the single localisation
1194	      \(A_{g a}\) and \((T_g)_h\) is \(T_{g a}\). Thus freeness of \((T_g)_h\) over
1195	      \((A_g)_h\) transports to freeness of \(T_{g a}\) over \(A_{g a}\) by the
1196	      iterated-\(\mathrm{Away}\)-tower descent \cref{lem:gf_away_tower_descent}. Set
1197	      \(f := g a \ne 0\) — the single product, \emph{not} a square — so that \(T_f\) is free
1198	      over \(A_f\).
1199	    \item \textbf{Splice.} The left end \(P_d^{\oplus m}\) of the sequence is a free
1200	      \(A\)-module (the polynomial ring \(P_d = A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) is \(A\)-free, hence
1201	      so is any finite direct power; note \(P_d^{\oplus m}\) is \emph{not} module-finite
1202	      over \(A\), but freeness is all the splice requires), and a free module stays free
1203	      under the localisation \(A \to A_f\); so \(P_d^{\oplus m}\) localises to a free
1204	      \(A_f\)-module. With both ends free over \(A_f\) after inverting \(f = g a\),
1205	      \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact} splices the localised short exact sequence to
1206	      conclude that the middle term \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\). Taking \(f = g a\)
1207	      completes the induction.
1208	  \end{enumerate}
1209	\end{proof}

1213	
1214	The following two lemmas package the finite \(A\)-module leaf in the two forms
1215	(flatness and module-finiteness) used by the dévissage chain.
1216	
1217	\begin{lemma}
1218	
1219	
1220

1227	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \(M_f\) is flat over \(A_f\).
1228	\end{lemma}

1234	\begin{lemma}

1255	\subsection{Geometric bridges for the affine assembly}
1256	\label{sec:gf_geometric_bridges}
1257	
1258	The geometric form of generic flatness is assembled from the algebraic core by
1259	restricting to a non-empty affine open of \(S\) and working patchwise over a finite
1260	affine cover of the preimage. Two genuinely geometric steps are isolated here as
1261	named bridges: the affine-local finiteness of the section module of a finite-type
1262	quasi-coherent sheaf (G1), feeding the algebraic core its finite-module input; and
1263	the flat-locality assembly (G3), promoting per-patch freeness to flatness of the
1264	restricted sheaf over the base open.
1265	
1266	\begin{lemma}[Finiteness is local on a finite spanning family]
1267	  \label{lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan}
1268	  \lean{Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite}
1269	  \mathlibok
1270	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Submodule}).}
1271	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(N\) an \(R\)-module, and \(t \subseteq R\) a
1272	  finite family with \(\mathrm{span}\,t = \top\) (the \(t\) generate the unit
1273	  ideal). Suppose that for each \(g \in t\) the localization \(N_g\) is a finite
1274	  module over the localization \(R_g\). Then \(N\) is a finite \(R\)-module.
1275	\end{lemma}
1276	
1277	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1278	[Half 1 helper — transfer of localized-module finiteness across models]
1279	  \label{lem:finite_localizedModule_transfer}
1280	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.finite_localizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule}
1281	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(S \subseteq R\) a multiplicative submonoid,
1282	  and \(M\) an \(R\)-module. Let \(R_S\) be an \(R\)-algebra that localizes \(R\)
1283	  at \(S\), and let \(N\) be an \(R\)-module carrying a compatible \(R_S\)-module
1284	  structure, together with an \(R\)-linear map \(\varphi : M \to N\) exhibiting
1285	  \(N\) as the localization of \(M\) at \(S\) (\(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\,S\,
1286	  \varphi\)). If \(N\) is a finite \(R_S\)-module, then the canonical localized
1287	  module \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,S\,M\) is a finite module over the canonical
1288	  localization \(\mathrm{Localization}\,S\).
1289	\end{lemma}
1290	
1291	\begin{proof}
1292	  The localization map \(\varphi\) provides an \(R\)-linear isomorphism
1293	  \(e : N \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,S\,M\) (universal property
1294	  of localized modules), and the algebra localization provides an \(R\)-algebra
1295	  isomorphism \(\psi : R_S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathrm{Localization}\,S\). These
1296	  are compatible: \(e\) is semilinear over \(\psi\), i.e.\ \(e(a \cdot x) =
1297	  \psi(a) \cdot e(x)\) for \(a \in R_S\), \(x \in N\) (both sides are pinned by
1298	  clearing a common denominator \(s \in S\), where the \(R\)-actions agree). A
1299	  finite \(R_S\)-spanning set \(T\) of \(N\) therefore maps under \(e\) to a
1300	  finite set whose \(\mathrm{Localization}\,S\)-span is all of
1301	  \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,S\,M\); hence the canonical model is finite over
1302	  \(\mathrm{Localization}\,S\).
1303	\end{proof}

1305	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1306	[Half 1 — G1 locality reduction over a finite basic-open cover]

1316	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (gluing step,
1317	  Algebra Lemma \texttt{lemma-cover}).}
1318	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(X\), and
1319	  \(W \subseteq X\) an affine open. Suppose \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) is a finite
1320	  family with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) (so the basic opens \(D(g)\),
1321	  \(g \in t\), cover \(W\)), and that for each \(g \in t\) the section module
1322	  \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) is finite over \(\Gamma(X, D(g))\). Then \(\Gamma(\F, W)\)
1323	  is a finite \(\Gamma(X, W)\)-module.

1325	
1326	\begin{proof}
1327	  \uses{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen, lem:finite_localizedModule_transfer,

1329	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (gluing step).}
1330	  By the local-on-a-spanning-family criterion \cref{lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan}
1331	  it suffices to show, for each \(g \in t\), that the localization of
1332	  \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) at \(g\) is finite over the localization
1333	  \((\Gamma(X, W))_g\). Fix \(g \in t\). The basic open \(D(g)\) is affine, and the
1334	  restriction \(\Gamma(X, W) \to \Gamma(X, D(g))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(X, D(g))\) as

1336	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}); moreover the section restriction
1337	  \(\Gamma(\F, W) \to \Gamma(\F, D(g))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) as the
1338	  localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,g)^{-1}\,\Gamma(\F, W)\)
1339	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}). Thus \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) is a

1349	\subsection{Transport engine for generating sections}
1350	
1351	The restriction-of-generators lemma below is assembled from a generic transport
1352	principle: a finite generating family of a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{M}\) pushes
1353	forward to a finite generating family of \(F(\mathcal{M})\) along any functor \(F\) of
1354	sheaves of modules that preserves colimits and carries the structure sheaf to the
1355	structure sheaf. The three blocks of this subsection package that principle (the
1356	generating family, its index type, and the finiteness bookkeeping). They are
1357	project-bespoke: Mathlib provides the analogous transport only for full presentations
1358	(with kernel data), not for bare generating families.
1359	
1360	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1361	[Transport of a generating family along a colimit-preserving functor]
1362	  \label{lem:genSections_map}
1363	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections.map}
1364	  Let \(F : \mathrm{Mod}\,R \to \mathrm{Mod}\,S\) be a functor of sheaves of modules
1365	  that preserves colimits and is equipped with an isomorphism
1366	  \(\eta : F(\OO_R) \xrightarrow{\sim} \OO_S\) carrying the unit (structure sheaf) of the
1367	  source to that of the target. Given a generating family \(\sigma\) of \(\mathcal{M}\)
1368	  with index set \(I = \sigma.I\) and epimorphism
1369	  \(\sigma.\pi : \OO_R^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{M}\), the images
1370	  \((\mathrm{mapFree}\,F\,\eta\,I)^{-1} \circ F(\sigma.\pi)\) form a generating family of
1371	  \(F(\mathcal{M})\) with the same index set \(I\). The map is an epimorphism because it
1372	  is the composite of an isomorphism with \(F(\sigma.\pi)\), and \(F\) preserves the
1373	  epimorphism \(\sigma.\pi\).
1374	\end{lemma}
1375	
1376	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1377	[The transported family keeps its index type]
1378	  \label{lem:genSections_map_I}
1379	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections.map_I}
1380	  \uses{lem:genSections_map}
1381	  The index set of the transported generating family of \cref{lem:genSections_map} equals
1382	  the index set of the original: \((\mathrm{map}\,\sigma\,F\,\eta)\!.I = \sigma.I\). This
1383	  holds definitionally.
1384	\end{lemma}
1385	
1386	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1387	[Finiteness survives transport]
1388	  \label{lem:genSections_map_isFiniteType}
1389	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections.map_isFiniteType}
1390	  \uses{lem:genSections_map, lem:genSections_map_I}
1391	  If the original generating family \(\sigma\) is of finite type (its index set is finite),
1392	  then so is the transported family \(\mathrm{map}\,\sigma\,F\,\eta\): the index type is
1393	  unchanged by \cref{lem:genSections_map_I}, hence still finite.
1394	\end{lemma}
1395	
1396	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1397	[Half 2, seam 1a — local generators restrict to a smaller open]
1398	  \label{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict}
1399	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_localGenerators_restrict}
1400	  \uses{lem:genSections_map, lem:genSections_map_I, lem:genSections_map_isFiniteType}
1401	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\F\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
1402	  Suppose that on an open \(Y \subseteq X\) the restriction \(\F|_Y\) is
1403	  globally generated by finitely many sections. Then for any open \(V \le Y\) the
1404	  restricted family — the images of those generators under the section-restriction
1405	  \(\Gamma(\F, Y) \to \Gamma(\F, V)\) — is again a finite generating family of
1406	  \(\F|_V\). Generation survives passage to the smaller open (a family that generates
1407	  every stalk over \(Y\) generates every stalk over \(V\)), and the index set, and hence
1408	  finiteness, is unchanged.
1409	\end{lemma}
1410	
1411	\begin{proof}
1412	  \uses{lem:genSections_map, lem:genSections_map_I, lem:genSections_map_isFiniteType}
1413	  A family of sections \(\{s_i\}_{i \in I}\) generates \(\F|_Y\) precisely when the
1414	  induced map \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \to \F|_Y\) is surjective on every stalk
1415	  \(\F_y\,(y \in Y)\). Restricting along the inclusion \(V \le Y\) the
1416	  generators map to \(\{s_i|_V\}_{i \in I}\), and for a point \(x \in V\) the stalk
1417	  \(\F_x\) and the comparison map agree with the value at \(x\) computed over \(Y\), since
1418	  stalks are insensitive to shrinking the ambient open. Hence the induced map
1419	  \(\OO_V^{\oplus I} \to \F|_V\) is surjective on every stalk over \(V\), so
1420	  \(\{s_i|_V\}_{i \in I}\) generates \(\F|_V\). The index set \(I\) is the same
1421	  finite set, so finiteness is preserved. Concretely the restricted family is obtained by
1422	  transporting the generating family along the (colimit-preserving, unit-preserving)
1423	  restriction functors via the transport engine \cref{lem:genSections_map}, whose index
1424	  type is unchanged (\cref{lem:genSections_map_I}) and which preserves finiteness
1425	  (\cref{lem:genSections_map_isFiniteType}).
1426	\end{proof}
1427	
1428	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1429	[Half 2, seam 1b — finite standard-basic-open subcover of an affine open]
1430	  \label{lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover}
1431	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover}
1432	  Let \(W\) be an affine open of a scheme \(X\), with the canonical identification
1433	  \(W \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\). Given an arbitrary open cover \(\{U_\alpha\}\) of \(W\),
1434	  there is a finite family \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with
1435	  \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) such that each basic open \(D(g)\) (\(g \in t\)) is
1436	  contained in some cover member \(U_\alpha\). In particular the \(D(g)\,(g \in t)\) form a
1437	  finite standard-basic-open subcover of \(W\) refining \(\{U_\alpha\}\).
1438	\end{lemma}
1439	
1440	\begin{proof}
1441	  Under \(W \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) the basic opens \(D(g)\,(g \in \Gamma(X, W))\) form a
1442	  basis of the topology (\(\mathrm{PrimeSpectrum.isBasis\_basic\_opens}\)). Hence each
1443	  cover member \(U_\alpha\) is a union of basic opens, and the collection of all basic
1444	  opens \(D(g)\) that are contained in some \(U_\alpha\) still covers \(W\). The affine
1445	  scheme \(W\) is quasi-compact, so finitely many of these basic opens
1446	  \(D(g_1), \dots, D(g_n)\) already cover \(W\); set
1447	  \(t = \{g_1, \dots, g_n\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\), a finite family, each \(D(g_i)\)
1448	  contained in some \(U_\alpha\) by construction. That the \(D(g_i)\) cover
1449	  \(W = \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) — i.e. no prime ideal contains all the \(g_i\) — is exactly
1450	  the condition \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\).
1451	\end{proof}
1452	
1453	\begin{lemma}[Half 2, seam 1c — finite generation equals a finite free epimorphism]
1454	  \label{lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1455	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1456	  Let \(Y\) be an open of a scheme and \(\F\) a sheaf of modules on \(Y\). Then
1457	  \(\F\) is globally generated by a finite family of sections if and only if there is a
1458	  finite index set \(I\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_Y\)-modules
1459	  \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F\). No quasi-coherence of \(\F\) is assumed:
1460	  the statement holds for arbitrary sheaves of modules on \(Y\), and applies in particular
1461	  to the restrictions \(\F|_{D(g)}\) arising in the cover assembly.
1462	\end{lemma}
1463	
1464	\begin{proof}
1465	  A finite family of sections \(\{s_i\}_{i \in I}\) of \(\F|_Y\) is the same datum as an
1466	  \(\OO_Y\)-linear map \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \to \F|_Y\) sending the \(i\)-th basis section to
1467	  \(s_i\). The family generates \(\F|_Y\) (the induced map is surjective on every stalk)
1468	  exactly when this map is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Thus a finite generating
1469	  family yields a finite-free epimorphism, and conversely the images of the basis sections
1470	  of any epimorphism \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_Y\) with \(I\) finite form a
1471	  finite generating family.
1472	\end{proof}
1473	
1474	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1475	[Half 2, seam 1 — finite type yields a finite cover of globally-generated affines]
1476	  \label{lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1477	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1478	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1479	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2101-L2107).
1480	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1481	  % "Assume $\widetilde M$ is a finite type $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. This means
1482	  %  there exists an open covering of $X$ such that $\widetilde M$ restricted to
1483	  %  the members of this covering is globally generated by finitely many sections.
1484	  %  Thus there also exists a standard open covering
1485	  %  $X = \bigcup_{i = 1, \ldots, n} D(f_i)$ such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$
1486	  %  is generated by finitely many sections."
1487	  \uses{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict, lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover,
1488	        lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1489	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (standard
1490	  cover of generated affines).}
1491	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F\) a sheaf of modules of finite type,
1492	  and \(W \subseteq X\) an affine open. Then there is a finite family
1493	  \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) such that for
1494	  each \(g \in t\) the basic open \(D(g)\) is affine and \(\F|_{D(g)}\) is globally
1495	  generated by finitely many sections; equivalently, for each \(g \in t\) there is
1496	  a finite index set \(I_g\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_{D(g)}\)-modules
1497	  \(\OO_{D(g)}^{\oplus I_g} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_{D(g)}\).
1498	\end{lemma}
1499	
1500	\begin{proof}
1501	  \uses{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict, lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover,
1502	        lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1503	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (standard cover of generated affines).}
1504	  Finite type of \(\F\) means that \(X\) has an open covering \(\{Y_\alpha\}\) on each
1505	  member of which \(\F\) carries a finite generating family of sections. The intersections
1506	  \(\{Y_\alpha \cap W\}\) form an open cover of the affine \(W\). By the finite standard
1507	  subcover refinement \cref{lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover} there is a finite family
1508	  \(t = \{g_1, \dots, g_n\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with
1509	  \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) and each basic open \(D(g_i)\) contained in some
1510	  \(Y_\alpha \cap W\) — in particular \(D(g_i) \subseteq W\) is affine, and \(D(g_i)\)
1511	  embeds by an open immersion into the member \(Y_\alpha\) on which \(\F\) is finitely
1512	  generated. By the restriction-of-generators lemma
1513	  \cref{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict} the finite generating family on \(Y_\alpha\)
1514	  restricts to a finite generating family of \(\F|_{D(g_i)}\), so \(\F|_{D(g_i)}\) is
1515	  globally generated by finitely many sections. Finally, by the free-epimorphism
1516	  reformulation \cref{lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}, this is equivalent to the existence
1517	  of a finite index set \(I_{g_i}\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_{D(g_i)}\)-modules
1518	  \(\OO_{D(g_i)}^{\oplus I_{g_i}} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_{D(g_i)}\). That the \(D(g_i)\)
1519	  cover \(W = \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) is exactly \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\). This is
1520	  Stacks' passage from the local-generation datum to a standard finite cover of generated
1521	  affines.
1522	\end{proof}

1524	\begin{lemma}[Affine $\widetilde{(-)}\dashv\Gamma$ adjunction with its $\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}$ counit]
1525	  \label{lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib}
1526	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction}
1527	  \mathlibok
1528	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}).}
1529	  On the affine scheme \(\Spec R\) the associated-sheaf functor
1530	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}\,R \to (\Spec R).\mathrm{Modules}\) is left adjoint to the
1531	  affine section functor \(\Gamma : (\Spec R).\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{Mod}\,R\) (Mathlib's
1532	  \(\mathrm{moduleSpecΓFunctor}\), evaluation of a sheaf of modules at the top open). The counit of
1533	  this adjunction is the natural transformation
1534	  \(\Gamma(-)\,\widetilde{\phantom{x}} \to \mathrm{id}\) whose component at a sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\)
1535	  is \(\mathcal{M}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, \top)} \to \mathcal{M}\), and
1536	  the left adjoint \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is faithful.
1537	\end{lemma}

1539	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1540	[Half 2, seam 2 — $\Gamma$ on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections]
1541	  \label{lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1542	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1543	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib,
1544	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
1545	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1546	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2106-L2107) — the implication
1547	  % "$\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ generated by finitely many sections $\Rightarrow$
1548	  % $M_{f_i}$ finitely generated" is exactly section-level surjectivity of a sheaf
1549	  % epi over an affine.
1550	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1551	  % "such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ is generated by finitely many sections.
1552	  %  Thus $M_{f_i}$ is finitely generated for each $i$."
1553	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (sections of a
1554	  generated sheaf over an affine).}
1555	  Let \(V\) be an affine open of a scheme, with \(B = \Gamma(X, V)\) and
1556	  \(V \cong \Spec B\), and let \(\pi : \mathcal{G} \to \F\) be an epimorphism of
1557	  quasi-coherent sheaves of modules over \(V\). Then the induced map on sections
1558	  \(\Gamma(\pi) : \Gamma(\mathcal{G}, V) \to \Gamma(\F, V)\) is a surjective
1559	  \(B\)-module map.
1560	\end{lemma}
1561	
1562	\begin{proof}
1563	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib,
1564	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
1565	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (sections of a generated sheaf over an
1566	  affine).}
1567	  Write \(V \cong \Spec B\) and let \(\Gamma\) be the affine section functor on
1568	  \((\Spec B).\mathrm{Modules}\), so that \(\Gamma(\pi) = \Gamma(\mathcal{G}, V) \to
1569	  \Gamma(\F, V)\) is the map in question. By \cref{lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib} the
1570	  associated-sheaf functor \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is left adjoint to \(\Gamma\), with counit the
1571	  natural transformation whose component at a sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is
1572	  \(\mathcal{M}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, V)} \to \mathcal{M}\).
1573	
1574	  Because \(\mathcal{G}\) and \(\F\) are quasi-coherent, the qcoh-sections descent
1575	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}, fed through the counit-iso assembly
1576	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}) makes both counit components
1577	  \(\mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) and \(\F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) isomorphisms. Naturality of the
1578	  counit on \(\pi\) gives the commuting square
1579	  \[
1580	    \widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)} \circ \mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1}
1581	      = \F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1} \circ \pi,
1582	    \qquad\text{equivalently}\qquad
1583	    \widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)}
1584	      = \F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1} \circ \pi \circ \mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}.
1585	  \]
1586	  The right-hand side is a composite of an isomorphism, the epimorphism \(\pi\), and an
1587	  isomorphism, hence is itself an epimorphism. Thus \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)}\) is an epimorphism
1588	  of sheaves of modules. Since the left adjoint \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is faithful, it reflects
1589	  epimorphisms; therefore \(\Gamma(\pi)\) is an epimorphism in \(\mathrm{Mod}\,B\), i.e. the
1590	  \(B\)-linear map \(\Gamma(\pi)\) is surjective.
1591	
1592	  This is the structural content of the vanishing of higher quasi-coherent cohomology on an
1593	  affine — the failure of \(\Gamma\) to be right exact would obstruct exactly this surjectivity —
1594	  obtained here directly from the affine adjunction without invoking a cohomology computation.
1595	\end{proof}

1668	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1669	[G1 base case — finite affine sections from a finite generating family]
1670	  \label{lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections}
1671	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections}
1672	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi, lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
1673	        lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
1674	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules of
1675	  finite type, and \(D \subseteq X\) an affine open. Suppose the restriction
1676	  \(\F|_D = (\mathrm{pullback}\,D.\iota)^{*}\F\) carries a finite generating family
1677	  \(\sigma\), with index set \(I = \sigma.I\). Then the section module
1678	  \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) is a finite module over \(\Gamma(X, D)\). This is the affine base
1679	  case of \(G1\): once the generators are in hand on an affine open, finiteness of
1680	  sections is purely an affine (Spec-side) statement.
1681	\end{lemma}
1682	
1683	\begin{proof}
1684	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi, lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
1685	        lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
1686	  The content is the transport of the finite generating datum
1687	  from the category of sheaves of modules over the affine open \(D\) to the affine
1688	  scheme \(\Spec \Gamma(X, D)\), where the affine base case applies. Write
1689	  \(R = \Gamma(X, D)\) and use the canonical identification
1690	  \(D \cong \Spec R\) (\(\mathrm{isoSpec}\)). The argument has three steps.
1691	
1692	  \emph{(a) Quasi-coherence on \(\Spec R\).} Transport \(\F|_D\) along
1693	  \(\mathrm{isoSpec}^{-1}\) to a sheaf of modules \(\F'\) on \(\Spec R\). Finite type of
1694	  \(\F\) is inherited by the restriction, and quasi-coherence of \(\F'\) is the assertion
1695	  that its counit \(\F'.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism; this is the affine
1696	  quasi-coherence criterion \cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ} fed by the basic-open
1697	  localization datum \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}.
1698	
1699	  \emph{(b) A finite free epimorphism over \(\Spec R\).} The generating family \(\sigma\)
1700	  furnishes an epimorphism \(\OO_D^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_D\). Transporting along
1701	  \(\mathrm{isoSpec}^{-1}\) and using that the pullback of the free module along an
1702	  isomorphism is again free (\((\mathrm{pullback}\,\mathrm{isoSpec}^{-1})^{*}\OO_D^{\oplus I}
1703	  \cong \OO_{\Spec R}^{\oplus I}\)), together with the identification of the free sheaf with
1704	  the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{N}\) of \(N = R^{\oplus I}\) (the associated-sheaf
1705	  functor preserves coproducts and \(\widetilde{R} \cong \OO_{\Spec R}\)), gives an
1706	  epimorphism \(\widetilde{N} \twoheadrightarrow \F'\) with \(N = R^{\oplus I}\) a finite
1707	  \(R\)-module (\(I\) finite).
1708	
1709	  \emph{(c) Identification of section modules.} The affine global-sections functor sends
1710	  \(\F'\) to \(\Gamma(\F', \top)\), which the transport identifies with \(\Gamma(\F, D)\)
1711	  as \(R = \Gamma(X, D)\)-modules.
1712	
1713	  Now the affine base case \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi}, applied to the finite-free
1714	  quasi-coherent epimorphism \(\widetilde{N} \twoheadrightarrow \F'\) over \(\Spec R\),
1715	  shows \(\Gamma(\F', \top)\) is a finite \(R\)-module; by (c) this is \(\Gamma(\F, D)\)
1716	  finite over \(\Gamma(X, D)\).
1717	\end{proof}

1972	/-- **Generic flatness, algebraic form** (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic
1973	Flatness"). Let `A` be a noetherian domain, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and
1974	`M` a finite `B`-module regarded as an `A`-module via the scalar tower
1975	`A → B → M`. Then there exists `f ∈ A`, `f ≠ 0`, such that
1976	`LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M` is free over `Localization.Away f`.
1977	
1978	Blueprint: `thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`. The finite-`A`-module case is the
1979	thin Mathlib wrapper `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`;
1980	the finite-type residue is the classical §4 dévissage (Noether normalisation +
1981	the polynomial-ring core, both proved above). -/
1982	theorem genericFlatnessAlgebraic
1983	    (A B M : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1984	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
1985	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
1986	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
1987	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1988	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
1989	  by_cases hAM : Module.Finite A M
1990	  · -- Primary route: `M` module-finite over `A` ⟹ the finite-module helper.
1991	    haveI := hAM
1992	    exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
1993	  · -- Surviving residue: `M` finite over the finite-type algebra `B` but not
1994	    -- module-finite over `A`. Dévissage over the noetherian ring `B`
1995	    -- (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`) with motive
1996	    -- `P N := ∃ f ≠ 0, Free A_f (N_f)`, each `B`-module `N` carrying its restricted
1997	    -- `A`-action through `algebraMap A B`. The subsingleton obligation discharges
1998	    -- from the torsion base case (L1); the short-exact obligation from the L3
1999	    -- splice; the `N ≅ B/𝔭` obligation is the genuine residual (it needs L4
2000	    -- finiteness + L5, the polynomial-ring core still under construction).
2001	    haveI : IsNoetherianRing B := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing A B
2002	    have key : letI : Module A M := Module.compHom M (algebraMap A B)
2003	        ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
2004	          Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
2005	      refine IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime B
2006	        (inferInstance : Module.Finite B M)
2007	        (motive := fun N _ _ _ => letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2008	          ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
2009	            Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N))
2010	        ?_ ?_ ?_
2011	      · -- subsingleton `N` ⟹ torsion base case (L1).
2012	        intro N _ _ _ _
2013	        letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2014	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2015	        refine GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A B N ?_
2016	        exact LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff.mpr fun m =>
2017	          ⟨1, Submonoid.one_mem _, by rw [Subsingleton.elim m 0, smul_zero]⟩
2018	      · -- `N ≅ B/𝔭` (domain quotient, finite-type over `A`): the genuine residual.
2019	        -- L4 (`exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`) and L5
2020	        -- (`exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial`) are now BOTH closed and
2021	        -- axiom-clean, so the remaining work here is pure assembly:
2022	        --   1. Set `C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal` (a finite-type `A`-algebra DOMAIN, via
2023	        --      `Ideal.Quotient.isDomain` + `Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective`), and
2024	        --      transport the goal from `N` to `C` along the `B`-linear (hence
2025	        --      `A`-linear) equiv `a`, since freeness through `LocalizedModule` is
2026	        --      preserved by an `A`-linear equivalence.
2027	        --   2. Split on `Subsingleton (C ⊗_A K)` (`K = Frac A`).  If torsion, close
2028	        --      with the L1 leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A B N`.
2029	        --   3. Otherwise apply L4 to get `g ≠ 0`, `n`, and an injective `A_g`-algebra
2030	        --      map `φ : A_g[X_n] → C_g` with `C_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)`
2031	        --      module-finite over `A_g[X_n]` (via `φ.toAlgebra`).  Feed `C_g` (as a
2032	        --      finite `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`-module) to L5 at base `A_g`, obtaining
2033	        --      `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with `(C_g)_h` free over `(A_g)_h`.
2034	        --   4. Bridge the ring-localisation `C_g = Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)`
2035	        --      to the module-localisation `LocalizedModule (powers g) C` (an
2036	        --      `IsLocalizedModule`-uniqueness iso), then descend the `A_g`-witness `h`
2037	        --      to a single `f ∈ A` via `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower`.
2038	        -- The only genuinely fiddly step is the ring↔module localisation bridge in (4);
2039	        -- everything else is direct application of the now-closed L1/L4/L5 leaves.
2040	        intro N _ _ _ p a
2041	        letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2042	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2043	        classical
2044	        -- Step 1. Pass to the domain quotient `C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal`, a finite-type
2045	        -- `A`-algebra domain. `a` transports the goal between `N` and `C`.
2046	        haveI hp : p.asIdeal.IsPrime := p.isPrime
2047	        haveI : IsDomain (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) := Ideal.Quotient.isDomain p.asIdeal
2048	        haveI : Algebra.FiniteType A (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) :=
2049	          Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective
2050	            (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ A p.asIdeal) (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ_surjective A p.asIdeal)
2051	        suffices hC : ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
2052	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal)) by
2053	          obtain ⟨f, hf, hCfree⟩ := hC
2054	          refine ⟨f, hf, ?_⟩
2055	          haveI := hCfree
2056	          -- `LocalizedModule (powers f) C ≃ₗ[A_f] LocalizedModule (powers f) N`,
2057	          -- the localisation functor applied to `a.symm`, transfers freeness.
2058	          exact Module.Free.of_equiv
2059	            (IsLocalizedModule.mapEquiv (Submonoid.powers f)
2060	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal))
2061	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) N)
2062	              (Localization.Away f) ((a.restrictScalars A).symm))
2063	        -- Step 2. Torsion / non-torsion split on `C_K = K ⊗_A C`.

2065	            Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal))
2066	        · -- Torsion: the L1 leaf with `B := M := C`.
2067	          exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A
2068	            (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) htors
2069	        · -- Step 3–4. Non-torsion: Noether normalisation (L4) + polynomial core
2070	          -- (L5) + the ring↔module bridge + descent (`away_tower`).
2071	          set C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal with hCdef

2075	            not_subsingleton_iff_nontrivial.mp htors

2077	            (LocalizedModule.equivTensorProduct (nonZeroDivisors A) C).injective.nontrivial

2081	            GenericFreeness.exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial A C hCK

2083	          letI := algBg
2084	          letI := φ.toAlgebra

2086	          haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away g) :=
2087	            IsLocalization.isDomain_of_le_nonZeroDivisors _
2088	              (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr (mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg))

2090	            IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing (Submonoid.powers g) _ inferInstance
2091	          haveI : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
2092	              (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)) Cg :=
2093	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun r => (φ.commutes r).symm
2094	          -- Polynomial core (L5) at base `A_g`: invert `h ∈ A_g` to free `C_g`.

2096	            GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial (Localization.Away g) n Cg
2097	          -- Step 4. Bridge `LocalizedModule (powers g) C ≃ₗ[A_g] C_g`. The ambient

2102	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun x => (hcompat x).symm
2103	          let e0 : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[A] Cg :=
2104	            IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers g)
2105	              (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom A C Cg).toLinearMap
2106	          let e : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[Localization.Away g] Cg :=
2107	            LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers g)

2144	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1 affine-local finiteness (Stacks 01PB)
2145	
2146	The geometric form of generic flatness (`genericFlatness`) consumes the
2147	affine-local finiteness of the section module of a finite-type quasi-coherent
2148	sheaf (blueprint G1, `lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`, Stacks Tag 01PB).
2149	This section builds the **locality reduction** for that statement: given a finite
2150	basic-open cover of an affine open `W` on each member of which the section module
2151	is module-finite, the section module on `W` is module-finite. It is assembled from
2152	the QUOT keystone `Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (gap2: the section
2153	restriction `Γ(F,W) → Γ(F, D(g))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers g)`) together with
2154	Mathlib's finite-generation gluing `Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite`.
2155	
2156	The remaining (genuinely geometric, Mathlib-absent) ingredient toward a complete
2157	G1 is the *base case*: on an affine where the finite-type generating sections of
2158	`F` are global, the section module is finite — i.e. the sheaf epimorphism
2159	`free I → F|_{D(g)}` (with `I` finite) is module-level surjective on global affine
2160	sections. That bridge (sheaf-epi ⟹ stalkwise surjective ⟹ module generation) is
2161	flagged in the file `task_results` handoff. -/

2163	/-- **Transfer of module finiteness across localized-module models.** If `Rₚ`
2164	localizes `R` at `S` and `φ : M →ₗ[R] N` exhibits `N` as the localization of `M`
2165	at `S` (with `N` an `Rₚ`-module compatibly), then finiteness of `N` over `Rₚ`
2166	transfers to finiteness of the *canonical* localized module `LocalizedModule S M`
2167	over `Localization S`. Used to feed the geometric section module `Γ(F, D(g))`
2168	(localized via the gap2 keystone over `Γ(X, D(g))`) into Mathlib's
2169	`Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite`, whose per-element hypothesis is phrased
2170	in the canonical `Localization.Away`/`LocalizedModule.Away` models. Project-local:
2171	Mathlib has the canonical-model finiteness instance (needing global finiteness) but
2172	no model-independent transfer. -/
2173	theorem finite_localizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule

2175	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
2176	    {N : Type*} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module Rₚ N] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ N]
2177	    (φ : M →ₗ[R] N) [IsLocalizedModule S φ] [Module.Finite Rₚ N] :
2178	    Module.Finite (Localization S) (LocalizedModule S M) := by
2179	  classical
2180	  let e : N ≃ₗ[R] LocalizedModule S M :=
2181	    IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S φ (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M)
2182	  let ψ : Rₚ ≃ₐ[R] Localization S := IsLocalization.algEquiv S Rₚ (Localization S)
2183	  have hsemi : ∀ (a : Rₚ) (x : N), e (a • x) = ψ a • e x := by
2184	    intro a x
2185	    obtain ⟨⟨r, s⟩, hrs⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S a
2186	    have hsa : (s : R) • a = (algebraMap R Rₚ) r := by
2187	      rw [Algebra.smul_def, ← hrs, mul_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_spec]
2188	    have hunit : IsUnit ((algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule S M))) (s : R)) :=
2189	      IsLocalizedModule.map_units (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M) s
2190	    have hbij := (Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp hunit

2194	    simp only [Module.algebraMap_end_apply]
2195	    rw [← map_smul e ((s:R)) (a • x)]
2196	    have hL : (s:R) • (a • x) = (r:R) • x := by
2197	      rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul Rₚ (s:R) (a • x), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def, hsa,
2198	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
2199	    have hR : (s:R) • (ψ a • e x) = (r:R) • e x := by
2200	      rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (Localization S) (s:R) (ψ a • e x), smul_smul,
2201	        ← Algebra.smul_def, ← map_smul ψ, hsa, ψ.commutes, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]

2209	    have := h (e.symm y) (by rw [hT]; trivial)
2210	    rwa [e.apply_symm_apply] at this
2211	  intro z hz
2212	  induction hz using Submodule.span_induction with
2213	  | mem z hz => exact Submodule.subset_span ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩

2220	family `t ⊆ Γ(X, W)` generates the unit ideal (so the basic opens `D(g)`, `g ∈ t`,

2225	restriction to `D(g)` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers g)`) — with `Γ(X, D(g))` the

2232	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]

2234	    (t : Finset Γ(X, W)) (ht : Ideal.span (t : Set Γ(X, W)) = ⊤)
2235	    (H : ∀ g ∈ t, Module.Finite Γ(X, X.basicOpen g) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g)) :

2237	  apply Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite t ht
2238	  intro g
2239	  letI : Module Γ(X, W) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g.val) :=
2240	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g.val))

2245	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F hW g.val

2250	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1 base case via the affine tilde–Γ adjunction
2251	
2252	The base case of blueprint G1 (`lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`, Stacks 01PB):
2253	on an affine `V ≅ Spec R`, a quasi-coherent sheaf globally generated by finitely
2254	many sections has a finite section module. The crux is **seam 2**
2255	(`gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi`): the affine global-sections functor `moduleSpecΓFunctor`
2256	sends a sheaf epimorphism of quasi-coherent modules to a *surjective* `R`-module
2257	map. This is the structural content of "H¹(affine, qcoh) = 0" delivered for free by
2258	Mathlib's affine `tilde.adjunction` counit (`fromTildeΓNatTrans`) — no cohomology
2259	build. Recipe: `analogies/gf-gamma-exact.md`. -/
2260	
2261	/-- **Seam 2 — `Γ` on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections.**
2262	For `G, F : (Spec R).Modules` with both tilde–Γ counits `IsIso` (the quasi-coherent
2263	hypothesis, supplied by `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict` /
2264	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`) and `π : G ⟶ F` an epimorphism of sheaves of
2265	modules, the affine global-sections map `(moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom` is a
2266	surjective `R`-linear map.
2267	
2268	Mechanism (no cohomology): the counit `fromTildeΓNatTrans` is natural in `π`, so
2269	`~(Γπ) = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ` — a composite of an iso, the epi `π`,
2270	and an iso, hence an epi. `tilde.functor R` is faithful, so it reflects epimorphisms;
2271	therefore `Γπ` is epi in `ModuleCat R`, i.e. surjective. Project-local: Mathlib's

2405	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — transport of generating sections along a functor
2406	
2407	`GeneratingSections.map` is the `GeneratingSections` analogue of Mathlib's
2408	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.mapGenerators`/`Presentation.map`. Given a
2409	colimit-preserving functor `F : SheafOfModules R ⥤ SheafOfModules S` together with a
2410	unit-iso `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, a (finite) generating family of `M` transports to a
2411	(finite) generating family of `F.obj M` with the *same* index type. This is the engine of
2412	seam 1a (`gf_localGenerators_restrict`): restriction of a sheaf of modules along an open
2413	immersion is such a functor (via the project's slice-to-geometric equivalence
2414	`overRestrictEquiv`, whose unit-iso is `overRestrictUnitIso`), so finite generation
2415	survives restriction. Project-local: Mathlib packages this transport only for full
2416	`Presentation`s (kernel data included), not for bare generating families. -/

2418	namespace SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections
2419	
2420	universe v₁ u₁ v₂ u₂
2421	
2422	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
2423	  [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
2424	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
2425	  {C' : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} C'] {J' : GrothendieckTopology C'} {S : Sheaf J' RingCat.{u}}
2426	  [HasSheafify J' AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J'.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
2427	  [J'.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
2428	
2429	/-- Transport a generating family of `M` along a colimit-preserving functor `F` carrying
2430	the unit to the unit (`η : F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`): the images
2431	`(mapFree F η σ.I).inv ≫ F.map σ.π` of the generators generate `F.obj M`, with the index
2432	type unchanged.
2433	
2434	The colimit-preservation hypothesis `hF` is taken as an *explicit* argument (rather than an
2435	instance) so that callers supply it directly: instance search for
2436	`PreservesColimitsOfSize` on the `def`-backed `Scheme.Modules` functors (e.g.
2437	`Scheme.Modules.pullback`) does not reliably fire through the `Modules` type abbreviation. -/
2438	noncomputable def map {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections)
2439	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2440	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2441	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2442	    (F.obj M).GeneratingSections where
2443	  I := σ.I
2444	  s := (F.obj M).freeHomEquiv ((letI := hF; SheafOfModules.mapFree F η σ.I).inv ≫ F.map σ.π)
2445	  epi := by
2446	    letI := hF
2447	    letI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{0, 0} F := preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink _
2448	    rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
2449	    haveI : Epi (F.map σ.π) := inferInstance
2450	    exact epi_comp _ _

2453	@[simp] theorem map_I {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections)
2454	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2455	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2456	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2457	    (map σ F η hF).I = σ.I := rfl
2458	
2459	/-- The transported generating family is of finite type whenever `σ` is (the index type is
2460	unchanged). Stated as a theorem with explicit `hF` since `map` takes its colimit-preservation
2461	witness explicitly. -/
2462	theorem map_isFiniteType {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections) [σ.IsFiniteType]
2463	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2464	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2465	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2466	    (map σ F η hF).IsFiniteType :=
2467	  ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite σ.I)⟩

The Lean signature only carries `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` (no `[F.IsFiniteType]`). The Lean is strictly more general: it requires only quasi-coherence plus an explicit finite generating family `σ` with `[σ.IsFiniteType]`, making the abstract `IsFiniteType` hypothesis superfluous. The blueprint is **over-specified**.
- **Proof follows sketch**: yes — the three-step structure (a) quasi-coherence transport via `isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec`, (b) generating family transport via `GeneratingSections.map`, (c) section comparison via `gammaPullbackImageIso` + semilinear transport with `module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` matches the proof sketch in the blueprint. Mathematical content is faithful.
- **notes**:
  - `\uses{}` lists `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen` as a direct dependency but the Lean proof does not call this lemma directly (appears at most transitively); the `\uses{}` list should be updated to replace it with the actual direct deps.
  - `\uses{}` is missing `module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` (called at step c) and the section-comparison isos `gammaPullbackImageIso` / `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_semilinear`.

### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}` (chapter: `lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`)

## Red flags

No `axiom` declarations. No suspect bodies (`:= True`, `:= rfl` on non-trivial claims). No excuse-comments on the three focus declarations. The `sorry` in `genericFlatness` is expected and marked correctly.

## Unreferenced declarations (informational)

### `module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` — **should be in blueprint** (major)

Lean lines 2573–2591:
```lean
theorem module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear {R R' : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing R']
    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {M' : Type*} [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R' M']
    (σ : R ≃+* R') (e : M ≃+ M') (he : ∀ (a : R) (x : M), e (a • x) = σ a • e x)
    [Module.Finite R M] : Module.Finite R' M'
```

This is a **substantive new helper**, explicitly noted in the docstring as "project-local" (Mathlib has `Module.Finite.equiv` for same-ring linear transport and `Module.Free.of_ringEquiv` for free transport across a ring iso, but neither covers the ring-iso + semilinear additive iso case). It is called inside `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections` at step (c) as the semilinear transport mechanism. The proof is non-trivial. The blueprint's proof sketch for `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections` references "a semilinear comparison" but does not give this helper its own block or `\lean{...}` pin.

This should have a named blueprint block (e.g. `lem:module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear`) in the G1 base-case section of the chapter.

The prose says "of finite type" (implying `IsFiniteType`) when the Lean correctly uses only `IsQuasicoherent` + the generating family. A prover following the blueprint would have imposed an unnecessary `IsFiniteType` hypothesis.
- **Generality**: **too narrow** for `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections`. Blueprint describes the lemma as if it requires `[F.IsFiniteType]`; the Lean proves the strictly more general statement (`[F.IsQuasicoherent]` only) that is also needed by `gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`.
- **Recommended chapter-side actions**:

  2. **Add** a `\begin{lemma}...\end{lemma}` block for `module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` in the G1 base-case section (before `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections`), with `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear}` and a one-sentence proof sketch ("transport `Module.Finite` via a ring isomorphism σ and a σ-semilinear additive isomorphism e").
  3. **Update** `\uses{}` for `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections`: remove `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen` (transitive only, not direct); add `lem:module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear`.

**Overall verdict**: Blueprint over-states one hypothesis for the G1 base-case lemma and is missing the semilinear transport helper block; Lean implementations are correct and more general than the blueprint describes — 3 declarations checked, 2 major findings (both blueprint-side, no Lean errors).
